title: 🔬 DORA × Copilot Cohort [v2]
description: Cross-analysis of DORA engineering metrics segmented by Copilot adoption cohort, revealing the impact of AI assistance on software delivery performance.

list.name: environment
list.label: Environment
list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 🔬 DORA × Copilot Cohort

**Supplementary Metrics — Copilot impact on DORA performance**

## What it Shows
This dashboard compares the four DORA metrics side-by-side for two cohorts: developers with an active Copilot seat versus those without. It surfaces whether Copilot usage correlates with faster lead times, more frequent deployments, or lower failure rates.

## Why it Matters
Cohort comparisons provide the strongest available signal (short of a controlled experiment) for Copilot's impact on engineering velocity and quality. If Copilot-active developers consistently show better DORA scores, it strengthens the case for broader adoption and investment.

> ⚠️ **Attribution caveat**: Cohort membership is determined by active seat status (last activity within 28 days) — not by confirmed Copilot use on specific PRs. Selection bias may exist if higher-performing developers are also more likely to adopt new tools.

## How to Interpret
- **Lead Time / Cycle Time**: Lower values for Copilot cohort suggest faster code delivery
- **PR throughput**: Higher weekly PR counts for Copilot cohort indicate productivity gains
- **Lines changed**: More lines per developer in Copilot cohort may reflect reduced friction writing code

## How to Use This Data
- Use this dashboard to build the business case for Copilot investment
- Combine with the **Copilot Adoption** dashboard to ensure the active cohort is large enough to be representative
- Track trends over time — short-term spikes may reflect novelty effects rather than sustained productivity gains

panels.description: DORA × Copilot Cohort — compares DORA metrics between Copilot-active and non-active developers.

panels.title: Change Lead Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot
panels.description:
### What it measures
Median change lead time (merge to deploy) for PRs authored by active Copilot users versus non-Copilot users.

### What insight it offers
If Copilot users have shorter lead times, it suggests AI-assisted development accelerates code review and deployment readiness.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Requires deployment-PR linkage via deployment_pull_requests
- Copilot user definition: active seat in the last 28 days

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Lead Time (hours)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1

panels.title: PR Cycle Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot
panels.description:
### What it measures
Median PR cycle time (open to merge) for Copilot active users versus non-Copilot users.

### What insight it offers
Shorter cycle times for Copilot users may indicate AI assistance helps produce cleaner code that passes review faster.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Does not filter by environment; all repositories included
- Cycle time includes both coding and review waiting time

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "PR Cycle Time (hours)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1

panels.title: Merged PRs by Cohort (weekly)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Weekly count of merged PRs split by Copilot active versus non-Copilot cohorts.

### What insight it offers
Shows whether Copilot users are maintaining or increasing their output share over time relative to non-Copilot peers.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Copilot cohort is fixed at current 28-day active seats, not historical
- Does not account for team size changes over time

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(*) AS "PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Lines Changed by Cohort (weekly)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Weekly total lines of code changed (additions + deletions) in merged PRs, split by Copilot cohort.

### What insight it offers
Higher lines changed per PR from Copilot users may indicate AI assistance boosts throughput. However, this should be balanced against PR quality metrics.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Requires PR additions/deletions to be stored in pull_requests table
- Very large PRs can skew weekly totals significantly

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  SUM(COALESCE(pr.additions, 0) + COALESCE(pr.deletions, 0)) AS "Lines Changed"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Change Fail Rate: Copilot vs Non-Copilot
panels.description:
### What it measures
Change failure rate for deployments linked to PRs by Copilot active users versus non-Copilot users.

### What insight it offers
Lower CFR for Copilot users may suggest AI-assisted code is less likely to cause incidents, possibly due to better inline error detection.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Incident detection uses 24-hour window heuristic
- Small sample sizes per cohort reduce statistical confidence

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
deploy_cohorts AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
),
failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  dc.cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT f.deployment_id)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT dc.deployment_id), 0) * 100, 1) AS "CFR (%)"
FROM deploy_cohorts dc
LEFT JOIN failures f ON f.deployment_id = dc.deployment_id
GROUP BY 1

panels.title: MTTR: Copilot vs Non-Copilot
panels.description:
### What it measures
Median MTTR for failure-recovery cycles in deployments associated with Copilot active versus non-Copilot PRs.

### What insight it offers
Faster recovery for Copilot teams may indicate AI assistance in diagnosing and fixing production issues or producing more reversible changes.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Requires deployment-PR linkage for cohort classification
- Low failure counts per cohort reduce statistical reliability

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
failure_deploys AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    d.environment,
    d.created_at AS failed_at,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort,
    LEAD(d.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY d.environment ORDER BY d.created_at) AS recovered_at
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Median MTTR (hours)"
FROM failure_deploys
WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1

panels.title: Copilot Seats Loaded
panels.description: Total rows in the copilot_seats table. Shows 0 in red when the Copilot data pipeline has not run successfully — check sync job logs for HTTP 403/404 warnings.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.title: Last Successful Sync
panels.description: Timestamp of the most recent sync job that completed with status=success. If this is empty or very old, trigger a new sync: POST http://localhost:3003/api/sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
